definitions.text: Nearest in place or position, having nothing similar intervening; adjoining.
examples: The man in the next bunk kept me awake all night with his snoring.
She lives a mile or two away, in the next village.

definitions.text: Most direct, or shortest or nearest in distance or time.
Nearest in order, succession, or rank; immediately following (or sometimes preceding) in order.
examples: Please turn to the next page.
On Wednesday next, I'm going to Spain.

definitions.text: Denotes the one immediately following the current or most recent one.
examples: Next week would be a good time to meet.

definitions.text: Closest in the future, or closest but one if the closest is very soon; of days, sometimes thought to specifically refer to the instance closest to seven days (one week) in the future.
examples: The party is next Tuesday; that is, not tomorrow, but eight days from now.
When you say next Thursday, do you mean Thursday this week or Thursday next week?

definitions.text: In a time, place, rank or sequence closest or following.
examples: They live in the next closest house.
It's the next best thing to ice cream.

definitions.text: So as to follow in time or sequence something previously mentioned.
examples: First we removed all the handles; next, we stripped off the old paint.
Financial panic, earthquakes, oil spills, riots. What comes next?
When we next meet, you'll be married.

definitions.text: On the side of; nearest or adjacent to; next to.
The one that follows after this one.
examples: Next, please, don't hold up the queue!
